post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 234 (Newbie Game 92)

Heavily inspired by fdas's ruleset
Important Rules:
Do not edit or delete posts
Do not post during night
Do not post if you are dead or if you are not in the game
Do not talk about anything related to this game outside this thread
Do not reference communications with the moderator
The Mafia can talk anytime!
Play to win

  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not attempt to impersonate the moderator
    • 1.10: Do not reference any moderator communication.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". As long as your vote looks like a vote on someone, it will be counted.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.8: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.9: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.10: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.11: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person(s) hanged (if any) are officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 240 hours and each night will be 48 hours.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: I will prod you via PM every 36-48 hours.
    • 3.4: If the mods grow tired of prodding you, you will be replaced. This is completely up to our discretion.
    • 3.5: Please declare any V/LA in bold. I will not prod you during this time, but you might get replaced if it is too long.
    • 3.6: If you want to be replaced, please do so via PM and do not declare it in thread.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (midwaybear) am the moderator of this game
    • 5.2: Blue-colored text is used for official votecounts by the moderators
    • 5.3: Red-colored text is used for mafia related posts.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: Blue-colored text is also used for anything otherwise.
    • 5.6: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.
  • Setup
    This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

    In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
    1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
    2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
    3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
    4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
    5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
    6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons

    Role PMs
    Quote:
    You are a Vanilla Townie.

    You have no special abilities. You win when the mafia are eliminated.

    Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
    Quote:
    You are a Town Jailkeeper.

    Each night, you may choose a player to jail. That player may not be killed that night, and also may not perform any actions. You win when the mafia are eliminated.

    Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
    Quote:
    You are a Town Cop.

    Each night you may investigate a player to learn their alignment (town/mafia). You win when the mafia are eliminated.

    Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
    Quote:
    You are a Town Doctor.

    Each night, you may choose a player to protect. That player cannot be killed for that night. You win when the mafia are eliminated.

    Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
    Quote:
    You are a 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie.

    The first time someone attempts to kill you at night, you will survive. You win when the mafia are eliminated.

    Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
    Quote:
    You are a Town Tracker.

    Each night you may choose a player to track, to learn who, if anyone, your target visited that night. You win when the mafia are eliminated.

    Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
    Quote:
    You are a Mafia Goon. Your teammate is <player>, a <role>. You may talk with your teammate here.

    Each night, you and your teammate may choose a player to kill, and who performs the action. You win when the town is eliminated, or nothing can prevent this.

    Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
    Quote:
    You are a Mafia Roleblocker. Your teammate is <player>, a Mafia Goon. You may talk with your teammate here.

    Each night, you and your teammate may choose a player to kill, and who performs the action. Additionally, each night, you may choose a player to roleblock. That player may not perform any actions that night. You win when the town is eliminated, or nothing can prevent this.

    Confirm by replying to this PM with a summary of your role.
  • Resources
    Newbie Guide
    Mafia University
  • Read all of the rules

post_rendered:
Hello everyone,

It occurred to me that this never actually gets explained, so let me give you the really, really basic rundown of how this game works.

Everyone here is either town, who wants to kill the mafia by voting them out, or mafia, who want to live long enough to kill the entire town. Townies don’t want to get voted out, because that’s more time that the mafia isn’t getting voted out, and the mafia doesn’t want to get voted out for obvious reasons. Town has the majority of votes, so the voting will be, for the most part, meant to kill the mafia.

This means that everyone wants others to think that they are town.

This means you shouldn’t take it too seriously when someone says “I’m town” or “I’m scum” (scum meaning mafia). If they say “I’m town”, well duh, anyone would say that, and if they say “I’m scum”, they’re clearly joking.

Now, for voting people out, or hanging.

Often, on the first day, newbies will say things like “We shouldn’t hang today because we have no information”. The problem is that if we don’t hang, the next day will be the exact same but with one town player killed by the mafia. Night kills are guaranteed to hit town. Hangs aren’t. That’s why we need to hang as many times as possible.

Lastly, what will we actually do for most of this game? The bread and butter of Mafia is the day phases, where we just... talk. The core idea is that you can often figure out if players are town or mafia just by reading their posts. This is also known as scumhunting. Scumhunting is how the town wins the game, and pretending to scumhunt as mafia is how you make yourself look like a townie, so it’s how you make yourself harder for the town to find.

How exactly do you scumhunt? It’s often hard to tell. Plenty of experienced players struggle in knowing what to look for. There are some common tells that people use, like being suspicious of people who try to blend in, or being suspicious of people who overreact, or not being suspicious of people trying hard to scumhunt.

Unfortunately it’s never that simple. Most scumhunting is just intuition. Any of the above things can be benign in some contexts, good looks in some, and bad looks in others. It’s all on a case by case basis. Why do you think this person wrote this post? If the explanations for a townie writing a post are better than the explanations for scum writing the same post, it might earn a townread. If vice versa, maybe a scumread. If you can’t tell, maybe the one post just doesn’t matter. There are a lot of those posts that just don’t matter.

If you have an idea about whether someone is town or scum, don’t be shy, just say it. I and the other experienced players may appear to shoot your ideas down, but don’t be discouraged when that happens. We (<names of experienced players>) are here to help you learn the game, and trial and error is how you get there.

The word “scumhunting” might make you think you’re only looking for scum, but looking for town is also helpful; if you find a townie, that’s one less person you can accuse falsely. Finding town helps find scum this way. The same applies in reverse; if you find scum, you can name people that they aren’t likely to be partnered with, hence finding more townies. If you do both at once, you do both better.

Before we go further, do not claim your role for no reason. If you are a power role (any role with some special ability), you want to stay hidden so that the mafia doesn't just shoot you on the first night. If you aren't, you will narrow the possibilities of who can be one by claiming, which will make it harder for power roles to hide. For now, only claim if you are about to be hanged and the deadline is close.

Oh btw I’m town. Oh btw I’m scum.

Let the games begin.



Imagine the time that could have been saved in this game with something like this.

"fdas is acting very scummy, so I think we should vote him because we have a higher chance of hitting scum."

"fdas is being a sussy baka so I think we should eject him because he'll probably be an amogus impostor."
